name: Geno Smith
headline: Veteran reunion in New York, but the arrow points down
body:
The advanced profile is flashing warning signs. Smith's 2025 in Las Vegas was a sharp fall from his Seattle peak: a minus-0.137 EPA per play, a 41.9% success rate, and 17 interceptions against just 19 touchdowns across 15 games, finishing as the QB20 with 173.9 fantasy points. That follows a 2024 line that, while productive at 4,320 yards, already showed his EPA flatlining near zero. He took 55 sacks behind a leaky front, and his deep-attempt rate sat at a conservative 8.7%, capping the ceiling that fantasy QBs need.

He returns to the Jets as the Week 1 starter, reuniting with the franchise that drafted him in 2013. Garrett Wilson, rookie pass-catchers Kenyon Sadiq and Omar Cooper Jr., plus Breece Hall give him real support. But at 35 with negative efficiency trends, he's strictly a streaming or bye-week backup. Let someone else draft him in single-QB formats.
